description_long: This is a square, wooden crate filled to the brim with several pieces of cooked meat, specifically drumsticks with visible white bone ends. The crate is constructed from dark brown planks, reinforced with diagonal wooden slats on each side for added strength. The meat pieces are piled high, some overflowing and resting against the crate, suggesting it is used for transporting or storing food items. The crate has no visible lid or cover, leaving the contents exposed and easily accessible. There are no handles, locks, or labeling features present on the exterior. The overall appearance is rustic and utilitarian, suitable for use in markets, storage areas, or outdoor urban settings.
description: A rustic wooden crate filled with cooked meat drumsticks, open-topped and reinforced with diagonal slats. The contents overflow slightly, and the box has no handles, locks, or labels.
description_view_0: Front view shows the crate with a diagonal slat and two drumsticks spilling out in front.
description_view_1: Left view reveals more drumsticks overflowing from the crate, with several pieces resting on the ground.
description_view_2: Back view displays the crate's rear panel and a dense pile of drumsticks inside, with no meat spilling out.
description_view_3: Right view highlights drumsticks protruding from the crate and a clear view of the diagonal slat.
attribute_placement_location: floor, market stall, storage area, garage
